tweets.text: the matrix doesn't have to be real, but it is a very empowering mental model
tweets.author: @merulox

replyThread.text:
if i do it today, then i will do it tomorrow, as such, if i do it today, then i'm guaranteed to succeed

and most of the cognitive bottlenecks that genuinely keep low-achievers stuck despite their best attempts, don't apply to me, and i have metrics 2 prove it

i am everything
replyThread.author: @merulox

replyThread.text:
anyone can be anything, but not everyone can learn the conviction that they can be anything within their lifetime. That's what separates us.

never had a role model, so i spent my life learning from the belief systems of those around me. A lot of my conviction is rooted in this.
replyThread.author: @merulox

replyThread.text: i had to start internalizing things like "most people don't do anything, most people are x", "i actually do things compared to these people" and other mental models used by high achievers while I was sitting on my ass rotting away, to slowly build the ego i needed to achieve
replyThread.author: @merulox

replyThread.text: oh, and don't get me started on what I learned from books and music
